stage: spec-phase update complete — 11-SPEC re-scored with security fold (SEC-01..04). Plans 11-01/02/03 exist but PREDATE the fold; next is replan/amend.

completed_tasks.name: Remapped codebase — 7 docs in .planning/codebase/ (STACK, INTEGRATIONS, ARCHITECTURE, STRUCTURE, CONVENTIONS, TESTING, CONCERNS)
Folded ALL of Phase 13 into Phase 11 as SEC-01..04; removed Phase 13 from roadmap + deleted its dir
Re-spec'd 11-SPEC.md: 10 reqs, ambiguity 0.18; SEC reqs + edge/prohibition rows; REQUIREMENTS.md SEC-01..04 + traceability; STATE roadmap-evolution

remaining_tasks.name: Re-plan Phase 11 to cover SEC-01..04 — existing plans 11-01/02/03 do not include the security work (universal AuthGuard, per-role on writes, JWT fail-loud, CORS)
Decide: amend 11-03 (handlers/integration) to absorb SEC, or add a dedicated SEC plan slice (11-04)
Commit the existing untracked plans (11-01/02/03) once reconciled with the new SPEC
Execute Phase 11 (migration repair → schema/seed → Rust domain+resolver → read/issue API → SEC hardening)

blockers.description: Plans 11-01/02/03 predate the 2026-06-23 SEC-01..04 fold — they cover RSRC-BE-01..06 only. Executing them as-is would ship the phase WITHOUT the security hardening the SPEC now requires.
blockers.workaround: Re-plan or amend before execution; SEC mostly lands in 11-03 handlers/integration + likely a dedicated slice.

blockers.description: UI plan-gate false-positives on this backend phase (frontend:true) because the repo has a frontend. Phase 11 is backend-only.

Migration-chain repair (RSRC-BE-06) is large/risky. First task MUST query the live _sqlx_migrations table to decide rename-in-place vs new compensating migration; repair must not break the already-migrated live dev DB (:15530).
blockers.workaround: Wave 0 task: SELECT version FROM _sqlx_migrations ORDER BY version;

decisions.decision: Fold ALL of Phase 13 security into Phase 11; remove Phase 13
decisions.rationale: User chose 'All of Phase 13'. Shares the backend trust boundary with RSRC-BE-04; SEC-03/04 touch rocket_setup.rs which the phase already modifies for migration repair

decisions.decision: RBAC bar = AuthGuard on EVERY handler (incl. messaging/vendor_relations) + role_has_permission on all writes/mutations + sensitive (audit) reads
decisions.rationale: User chose pragmatic-demo bar over per-key-on-every-handler; reuses existing role_has_permission + AuthGuard, no new framework

decisions.decision: JWT fail-loud everywhere — remove hardcoded fallback; startup aborts when JWT_SECRET unset/empty; tests + docker-compose.dev + .env set it
decisions.rationale: User chose 'Fail-loud, env everywhere' over cfg(not(test)) gating
User chose single dev origin over env-configurable list

decisions.decision: (prior) Standalone TEXT IDs for the 8 tables, NOT FKs to organizations/person
decisions.rationale: organizations.id/person.id are SERIAL ints; seed uses string labels with no 1:1 map

decisions.decision: (prior) Embedded idempotent seed migration hand-ported from seed.ts; resolver pure module; golden-fixture parity; aggregate GET /api/digital-resources/world; issue actor from AuthGuard person->org; --skip-ui

next_action: Re-plan Phase 11 to cover SEC-01..04. The 3 existing plans (11-01 migration+schema, 11-02 resolver+models, 11-03 handlers+integration) cover RSRC-BE-01..06 but NOT the security fold. Decide: amend 11-03 to absorb SEC-01..04 + add a SEC slice, or re-run /gsd-plan-phase 11 --skip-ui and let it incorporate the new SPEC. Read 11-SPEC.md reqs 6-9 first.
context_notes: This session: codebase remap -> surfaced live security holes (CONCERNS.md) -> added Phase 13 -> user chose to fold ALL of it into Phase 11 -> re-spec'd 11-SPEC at ambiguity 0.18 with SEC-01..04. The prior deep planning session's plans (11-01/02/03) are still good for the resource-backend work but are now INCOMPLETE vs the SPEC — they don't touch messaging/vendor_relations AuthGuard, per-role write gating, JWT fail-loud, or CORS. SEC reuses existing primitives (AuthGuard, role_has_permission helper in src/shared/rbac.rs). rocket_setup.rs:22-23 (JWT fallback) + :58/:65 (CORS all + credentials) are the exact edit sites. messaging/handlers.rs + vendor_relations/handlers.rs are the two unguarded domains. Open question for execution: does the Rust resolver stub zoneAdvisory:null or fully port zone resolution.
